_note: Industry feed: Space / EO / climate / maritime news in dry insider voice. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: Spanish EO startup Xoople raises $130M Series B, signs L3Harris contract to build sensors for constellation
items.snark: Another nine-figure round for a company promising to map the Earth for AI, this time with L3Harris building the actual hardware.

items.headline: US government requested Planet voluntarily withhold imagery across broad Middle East region
items.snark: The defense paradox is no longer theoretical when the most open commercial model in EO gets an indefinite withhold request.

items.headline: BlackSky, Planet, Satellogic push sub-minute image latency as government customers demand real-time intel
items.snark: Two years ago the ask was ninety minutes, now it is minutes, and nobody is pricing for the RF ground station density required.

items.headline: Vantor announces two new EO constellations with 20cm and 40cm resolution, partners with Windward on maritime tracking
items.snark: Even the resolution leader now accepts the market wants both fidelity and frequency, which is another way of saying capex is about to double.

items.headline: Global Fishing Watch pushes mandatory vessel ownership transparency at FAO fisheries meeting in Reykjavik
items.snark: Ultimate beneficial ownership has gone from voluntary aspiration to the center of the policy conversation in thirty-six months.

items.headline: Global Fishing Watch 2025 annual report: entire industrial fishing fleet now mapped, 45M vessel detections from 291M sq km of satellite imagery
items.snark: GFW crossed the finish line on mapping every industrial vessel and still has five years to meet the 2030 goal of all human ocean activity.

items.headline: FCC grants AST SpaceMobile commercial authorization for direct-to-device service using MNO spectrum
items.snark: AST gets the full SCS license with 124 satellites due by August 2030, assuming Blue Origin can deliver them to the right orbit this time.

items.headline: True Anomaly raises $650M Series D at $2.2B valuation for Golden Dome interceptor satellites
items.snark: One of the largest private raises ever for space domain awareness just moved from observation into kinetic mission territory.

items.headline: Payload reports launch capacity crunch as customers struggle to manifest on SpaceX through integrators
items.snark: HawkEye 360 thought the launch thing was solved by now, but integrators buying up all capacity makes controlling your own ride the new competitive moat.

items.headline: Inside Climate News: Next El Niño could drive global temps to 1.7°C, potentially locking Earth past 1.5°C threshold permanently
items.snark: Hansen says a moderately strong El Niño in the next twelve to eighteen months pushes 1.7°C and we probably do not cool back below 1.5°C afterward.
